verdict.summary: Guide has several major regulatory errors: wrong UPAC thresholds, misleading licensing/registration wording, obsolete legal source, false net-metering statement, unreliable subsidy claims, and incorrect tax treatment of surplus electricity.

issues.current: UPAC ... до 6.9 кВт — без licenсии (registo в DGEG бесплатно)
issues.problem: The 6.9 kW threshold is misleading. In the current UPAC regime, the key prior-control thresholds are not 6.9 kW: very small units are exempt, units above the exemption threshold and up to 30 kW generally require prior communication/registration, and larger units require registration/certificate or licence depending on capacity. 6.9 kW is commonly confused with VAT/old microgeneration-style thresholds, not the general 'no licence' UPAC threshold.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "UPAC residential обычно не требует лицензии, но пороги другие: до 700 W — generally exempt from prior control if no grid injection; >700 W and ≤30 kW — mera comunicação prévia / registration via DGEG/SERUP; >30 kW — heavier prior-control requirements. Check current DGEG/SERUP rules before installation."

issues.current: UPAC до 6.9 кВт (residential)
issues.problem: Presents 6.9 kW as the residential/no-licence category, which is not the correct legal category for UPAC prior control.
issues.suggestion: Rename to "Residential UPAC ≤30 kW" and explain that most home systems fall under prior communication rather than licensing, while very small systems may be exempt.

issues.current: UPAC > 6.9 кВт
issues.problem: Creates a false regulatory split at 6.9 kW. A 7 kW or 10 kW residential UPAC is not automatically in a different licensing class merely because it exceeds 6.9 kW.
issues.suggestion: Use legal thresholds instead: "UPAC >30 kW" for systems requiring more formal registration/certification, and mention >1 MW licensing where relevant.

issues.current: DL 162/2019 — autoconsumo
issues.problem: DL 162/2019 is not the current primary framework in the way the guide implies. The electricity-sector framework was replaced/consolidated by Decreto-Lei 15/2022, which is the key legal source for current UPAC/self-consumption rules.
issues.suggestion: Add/replace with: "Decreto-Lei n.º 15/2022, de 14 de janeiro — Sistema Elétrico Nacional / autoconsumo" and use it as the main legal source.

issues.current: Для small UPAC — net metering (excess счётчик скидка) проще
issues.problem: Portugal does not have simple residential net metering where exported kWh are offset one-for-one against consumption. Surplus is either injected without remuneration or sold under a contract with a supplier/aggregator, usually at a much lower €/kWh than retail.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Net metering в смысле 1:1 зачёта в Portugal нет. Чтобы получать деньги за excedente, нужен bidirectional smart meter and sale contract with a comercializador/agregador; otherwise surplus may be injected without payment."

issues.current: Программа Fundo Ambiental субсидирует до 70% (€2 500 max).
issues.problem: Misleading as a standing 2026 rule. Fundo Ambiental calls are program-specific, budget-limited and not permanently open; percentages, caps, eligible equipment and deadlines change by notice. A blanket '2026 = 70%, €2,500 max' needs a specific 2026 aviso, otherwise it is unsafe.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Fundo Ambiental grants are not guaranteed annually; check the open Aviso on fundoambiental.pt. Past residential efficiency calls had specific caps and reimbursement rates, but 2026 amounts depend on the active programme."

issues.current: Stand 2026: до 2 500 € или 70% от cost (whichever lower)
issues.problem: States a fixed 2026 subsidy without citing the specific Fundo Ambiental notice. This is likely outdated or unverifiable; the guide's source is only the homepage, not a call document.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Amounts depend on the active Fundo Ambiental Aviso; do not budget the project assuming a subsidy until your application is approved."

issues.current: Лимитированный budget — open в 1 квартал, кончается за недели
issues.problem: Overgeneralised. Fundo Ambiental programmes do not reliably open in Q1 every year, and deadlines/budgets differ by call.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Calls open irregularly and have their own deadlines, budgets and eligibility rules; monitor Fundo Ambiental and the Diário da República."

issues.current: Excedente sale: облагается 28% налог
issues.problem: Misleading tax statement. For individuals, income from sale of surplus electricity from UPAC is not simply taxed at 28% in all cases. There is a specific IRS exclusion for small surplus sales up to a legal limit, and VAT obligations depend on turnover/exemption status. The usual residential case may have no IRS tax if within the exemption.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Excedente sale: for individuals, small UPAC surplus income may be excluded from IRS up to the statutory annual limit; above that, or for business activity, tax/VAT rules apply. Confirm with an accountant/comercializador."
issues.source: Código do IRS, art. 12; Lei do Orçamento do Estado 2023 changes for UPAC surplus income

issues.current: IVA 6% (reduced) на residential UPAC ≤ 6.9 кВт с 2023
issues.problem: The wording is likely wrong on both the threshold and timing. The reduced VAT rule for supply/installation of solar panels is in the VAT List I framework and is not generally described as 'residential UPAC ≤6.9 kW'. The guide also contradicts itself by saying elsewhere 'IVA 6% reduced (с 2024)'.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "IVA reduzido may apply to supply and installation of photovoltaic/solar panels under the current VAT List I wording; confirm invoice treatment with the installer. Do not present 6.9 kW as the VAT threshold unless citing the exact current legal text."
issues.source: Código do IVA, Lista I, verba 2.37

issues.current: IVA 6% reduced (с 2024) для residential
issues.problem: Internal contradiction with tax section, which says 'с 2023'. The start date and eligibility are not consistently stated.
issues.suggestion: Use one verified statement tied to the current VAT provision, without giving a year unless necessary.

issues.current: Smart meter установка от EDP / провайдера
issues.problem: Wrong actor. In mainland Portugal the distribution network operator is E-REDES, not the electricity supplier such as EDP Comercial/Galp/Endesa/Iberdrola. Smart meter/bidirectional metering is handled through the network operator process, often triggered via the supplier/installer.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Bidirectional smart meter / configuration is handled by the distribution network operator, usually E-REDES in mainland Portugal; the supplier/comercializador is separate."

issues.current: Atestado de residência (сметы from electric provider)
issues.problem: This is confused and likely wrong. Atestado de residência is a parish residence certificate and is not a standard technical document for UPAC registration. The relevant electricity identifier is usually the CPE from the electricity bill, plus owner/tenant authorization and installation data.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Electricity bill with CPE, NIF/ID, property owner authorization if not owner, technical data of panels/inverter, installer declarations, and condominium approval where common parts are used."

issues.current: Если кондоминиум против: суд (Lei 79/2017 — право на autoconsumo)
issues.problem: This is unsafe legal advice. There is no simple individual right allowing a flat owner/tenant to override condominium refusal by citing Lei 79/2017. Use of common roof/common parts is governed by condominium law and assembly rules; collective self-consumption has its own procedures.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "If panels use common parts, get condominium assembly approval under condominium rules. If refused, consult a lawyer; do not assume an automatic statutory right to install on common roof."
issues.source: Código Civil condominium rules; Decreto-Lei 15/2022 collective self-consumption regime

issues.current: Lei 79/2017 — tenants have right to renewable energy installations с разумными conditions от арендодателя
issues.problem: Likely false/misleading. Tenants generally need landlord consent for alterations/installations affecting the property, and there is no broad tenant right stated this way in the cited law.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Tenants need written landlord consent for any fixed installation; for removable balcony plug-in systems also check lease, condominium rules and electrical safety requirements."

issues.current: Тенanted property panels: могут забрать с собой при выезде, но обычно становятся owner property после 5 лет
issues.problem: No general Portuguese rule that tenant-installed solar panels become the landlord's property after 5 years. Fixtures/improvements depend on the lease, consent, removability and civil-law rules on improvements.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Who owns the panels and whether they can be removed must be agreed in writing with the landlord before installation."

issues.current: UPP — Unidade de Pequena Produção
issues.problem: UPP is presented as an ordinary current option, but the small-production regime has been superseded/absorbed by the current self-consumption and electricity-sector framework. For new projects, the guide should focus on UPAC and sale of surplus/production under the current DL 15/2022 regime rather than old UPP terminology.
issues.suggestion: Rewrite as: "Pure production/sale to grid is a separate electricity-production project under current SEN rules and is not the normal residential UPAC route; check DGEG/ERSE requirements and grid capacity."

issues.current: Property value: добавляет 5-10% к стоимости недвижимости
issues.problem: Unsupported and too categorical. The added value depends heavily on EPC rating, battery, age of equipment, warranties, roof status and buyer preferences. 5-10% is not a reliable Portugal-specific rule.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "May improve attractiveness/EPC-related perception, but resale-value uplift is case-specific and not guaranteed."

issues.current: CO2 reduction: ~3 тонны/год от 5 кВт system
issues.problem: Likely overstated for Portugal's relatively low-carbon grid. A 5 kWp system producing roughly 7,000-8,000 kWh/year would avoid materially less than 3 tCO2/year using recent Portuguese grid emission factors.
issues.suggestion: Use a softer range: "roughly 1-2 tCO2/year depending on generation and the emission factor used" or cite a current emission factor.
issues.source: Portuguese grid emission factors; APREN/REN/DGEG energy statistics

issues.current: 3 кВт (квартира): 3 500-5 500 € (8-10 панелей...)
issues.problem: Panel count is inconsistent with modern module wattages. 8-10 panels at 430-550 W is about 3.4-5.5 kWp, not 3 kW. The guide elsewhere says panels are 260-450 W, which is outdated for 2026.
issues.suggestion: Write: "3 kWp: usually about 6-8 modern panels, depending on module wattage" and update panel wattage range to roughly 400-600 W for current residential modules.
issues.source: Current residential PV module market specifications

issues.current: Solar panels (260-450 W каждая)
issues.problem: Outdated range for 2026; 260 W panels are old stock, and common residential panels are often 400 W+.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Solar panels, typically ~400-600 W each for current residential installations."
issues.source: Current residential PV module market specifications

issues.current: panel brand (LG, JA Solar, Trina)
issues.problem: LG exited the solar panel manufacturing business, so listing LG as a normal 2026 quote brand is outdated.
issues.suggestion: Replace LG with current common brands or say "panel brand and warranty" without naming LG.
issues.source: LG Electronics announcement ending solar panel production

issues.current: Excess solar production injected в grid → компенсация от вашего provider (EDP, Galp, Endesa, Iberdrola)
issues.problem: Not necessarily from 'your' existing provider. Surplus can be sold to a supplier/aggregator under a separate contract, and not all suppliers offer the same buyback terms.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Surplus can be sold to a comercializador/agregador under a specific contract; it may be your current supplier or another market participant."

missing_topics: Current UPAC prior-control thresholds: exemption, mera comunicação prévia, registration/certificate, licensing
SERUP/DGEG registration portal and required technical documents, including CPE
Need for bidirectional meter and role of E-REDES versus supplier/comercializador
No 1:1 net metering in Portugal; surplus sale requires contract and may otherwise be unpaid
Current IRS/VAT treatment of small surplus electricity sales, including the IRS exclusion threshold
Condominium approval rules for use of common roof/common parts and collective self-consumption options
Landlord written consent and lease terms for tenant installations
Fundo Ambiental grants are call-specific; cite the exact Aviso rather than generic caps
Grid-connection/export limitations and possible zero-injection configuration
Warranty distinctions: product warranty, performance warranty, inverter warranty, workmanship warranty
